papers.title_en: Nonlinear backstepping with saturation for low-thrust station-keeping of libration point orbits
papers.authors: António Nunes, Sérgio Brás, Pedro Batista
papers.evidence: 地月系统平动点轨道的低推力驻留控制
papers.tldr: 本文针对地月系统平动点轨道的低推力驻留问题，提出了一种考虑执行器饱和的非线性反步控制方法。该方法在高保真动力学模型下实现了几乎全局一致指数稳定，并通过李雅普诺夫理论进行了严格证明。研究探讨了饱和阈值、控制增益与偏差之间的关系，并提出了增益优化方案。通过蒙特卡洛仿真验证了该控制器在存在操作误差和外部扰动下的鲁棒性，特别适用于典型电力推进系统的饱和约束场景。
papers.abstract_en: This paper presents a novel nonlinear backstepping control law for continuous, low-thrust station-keeping in the Earth-Moon system. Quasi-periodic libration point orbits are targeted under a high-fidelity model of the dynamics. Almost global uniform exponential stability guarantees are attained, as shown through Lyapunov's stability theory. Saturation of the actuators is formally included in the controller design, such that these guarantees hold even in the event of saturation. The relationship between saturation threshold, control gains, and deviation is studied and an optimal procedure for gain selection is discussed. The control solution is tested numerically through a Monte Carlo analysis over representative application cases, subject to operational errors, constraints, and external perturbations. Station-keeping under actuation saturation is validated considering a conservative threshold for typical electric propulsion systems.

papers.title_en: SpaceMind: A Modular and Self-Evolving Embodied Vision-Language Agent Framework for Autonomous On-orbit Servicing
papers.authors: Aodi Wu, Haodong Han, Xubo Luo, Ruisuo Wang, Shan He, Xue Wan
papers.evidence: 自主在轨服务与3D空间推理
papers.tldr: SpaceMind是一个面向自主在轨服务的模块化、自进化具身视觉语言模型（VLM）智能体框架。它通过技能模块动态路由、MCP工具配置和可注入推理模式，实现了知识与推理的解耦。该框架利用MCP-Redis接口支持仿真与实体的无缝切换，并通过技能自进化机制在无需微调的情况下提升性能。实验证明其在复杂卫星任务中具有极高的成功率和鲁棒性。
papers.abstract_en: Autonomous on-orbit servicing demands embodied agents that perceive through visual sensors, reason about 3D spatial situations, and execute multi-phase tasks over extended horizons. We present SpaceMind, a modular and self-evolving vision-language model (VLM) agent framework that decomposes knowledge, tools, and reasoning into three independently extensible dimensions: skill modules with dynamic routing, Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools with configurable profiles, and injectable reasoning-mode skills. An MCP-Redis interface layer enables the same codebase to operate across simulation and physical hardware without modification, and a Skill Self-Evolution mechanism distills operational experience into persistent skill files without model fine-tuning. We validate SpaceMind through 192 closed-loop runs across five satellites, three task types, and two environments, a UE5 simulation and a physical laboratory, deliberately including degraded conditions to stress-test robustness. Under nominal conditions all modes achieve 90--100% navigation success; under degradation, the Prospective mode uniquely succeeds in search-and-approach tasks where other modes fail. A self-evolution study shows that the agent recovers from failure in four of six groups from a single failed episode, including complete failure to 100% success and inspection scores improving from 12 to 59 out of 100. Real-world validation confirms zero-code-modification transfer to a physical robot with 100% rendezvous success. Code: https://github.com/wuaodi/SpaceMind

papers.title_en: CT-VIR: Continuous-Time Visual-Inertial-Ranging Fusion for Indoor Localization with Sparse Anchors
papers.evidence: 用于定位的连续时间视觉-惯性-测距融合
papers.tldr: 针对视觉惯性里程计（VIO）在长距离运行中存在漂移且超宽带（UWB）测距在稀疏基站下易退化的问题，本文提出了一种基于连续时间状态估计的视觉-惯性-测距（VIR）融合定位方法CT-VIR。该方法利用B样条参数化轨迹，通过预处理构建虚拟基站并剔除异常值，在滑动窗口内联合优化控制点，有效解决了多传感器异步采样下的高精度定位与轨迹一致性问题。
papers.abstract_en: Visual-inertial odometry (VIO) is widely used for mobile robot localization, but its long-term accuracy degrades without global constraints. Incorporating ranging sensors such as ultra-wideband (UWB) can mitigate drift; however, high-accuracy ranging usually requires well-deployed anchors, which is difficult to ensure in narrow or low-power environments. Moreover, most existing visual-inertial-ranging (VIR) fusion methods rely on discrete time-based filtering or optimization, making it difficult to balance positioning accuracy, trajectory consistency, and fusion efficiency under asynchronous multi-sensor sampling. To address these issues, we propose a spline-based continuous-time state estimation method for VIR fusion localization. In the preprocessing stage, VIO motion priors and UWB ranging measurements are used to construct virtual anchors and reject outliers, thereby alleviating geometric degeneration and improving range reliability. In the estimation stage, the pose trajectory is parameterized in continuous time using a B-spline, while inertial, visual, and ranging constraints are formulated as factors in a sliding-window graph. The spline control points, together with a small set of auxiliary parameters, are then jointly optimized to obtain a continuous-time trajectory estimate. Evaluations on public datasets and real-world experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and practical potential of the proposed approach.

papers.title_en: Accelerating Transformer-Based Monocular SLAM via Geometric Utility Scoring
papers.authors: Xinmiao Xiong, Bangya Liu, Hao Wang, Dayou Li, Nuo Chen, Andrew Feng, Mingyu Ding, Suman Banerjee, Yang Zhou, Zhiwen Fan
papers.evidence: 单目SLAM与建图的几何效用评分
papers.tldr: 针对基于几何大模型（GFM）的单目SLAM系统计算开销大的问题，本文提出LeanGate轻量级门控网络。该方法通过在重型特征提取前预测帧的几何效用得分，提前剔除冗余帧，在保持精度的同时将端到端吞吐量提升5倍，显著降低了计算冗余并优化了系统效率。
papers.abstract_en: Geometric Foundation Models (GFMs) have recently advanced monocular SLAM by providing robust, calibration-free 3D priors. However, deploying these models on dense video streams introduces significant computational redundancy. Current GFM-based SLAM systems typically rely on post hoc keyframe selection. Because of this, they must perform expensive dense geometric decoding simply to determine whether a frame contains novel geometry, resulting in late rejection and wasted computation. To mitigate this inefficiency, we propose LeanGate, a lightweight feed-forward frame-gating network. LeanGate predicts a geometric utility score to assess a frame's mapping value prior to the heavy GFM feature extraction and matching stages. As a predictive plug-and-play module, our approach bypasses over 90% of redundant frames. Evaluations on standard SLAM benchmarks demonstrate that LeanGate reduces tracking FLOPs by more than 85% and achieves a 5x end-to-end throughput speedup. Furthermore, it maintains the tracking and mapping accuracy of dense baselines.

papers.title_en: LEADER: Learning Reliable Local-to-Global Correspondences for LiDAR Relocalization
papers.authors: Jianshi Wu, Minghang Zhu, Dunqiang Liu, Wen Li, Sheng Ao, Siqi Shen, Chenglu Wen, Cheng Wang
papers.evidence: 三维环境中的LiDAR重定位与6自由度位姿估计
papers.tldr: 针对LiDAR重定位中回归方法易受噪声和离群点干扰的问题，本文提出了LEADER框架。该框架通过鲁棒的投影几何编码器提取多尺度特征，并引入截断相对可靠性损失函数来建模点云预测的模糊性，从而过滤不可靠预测。在Oxford RobotCar和NCLT数据集上的实验表明，该方法显著降低了定位误差，相比现有技术分别实现了24.1%和73.9%的性能提升。
papers.abstract_en: LiDAR relocalization has attracted increasing attention as it can deliver accurate 6-DoF pose estimation in complex 3D environments. Recent learning-based regression methods offer efficient solutions by directly predicting global poses without the need for explicit map storage. However, these methods often struggle in challenging scenes due to their equal treatment of all predicted points, which is vulnerable to noise and outliers. In this paper, we propose LEADER, a robust LiDAR-based relocalization framework enhanced by a simple, yet effective geometric encoder. Specifically, a Robust Projection-based Geometric Encoder architecture which captures multi-scale geometric features is first presented to enhance descriptiveness in geometric representation. A Truncated Relative Reliability loss is then formulated to model point-wise ambiguity and mitigate the influence of unreliable predictions. Extensive experiments on the Oxford RobotCar and NCLT datasets demonstrate that LEADER outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving 24.1% and 73.9% relative reductions in position error over existing techniques, respectively. The source code is released on https://github.com/JiansW/LEADER.

papers.title_en: Layered Control of Partially Observed Stochastic Systems
papers.authors: Charis Stamouli, Anastasios Tsiamis, George J. Pappas
papers.evidence: 部分观测随机系统的分层控制
papers.tldr: 针对大规模系统在部分观测和随机噪声环境下的复杂性管理问题，本文提出了一种分层控制框架。该框架通过引入新型的“随机模拟函数”，确保了相邻层级系统间预期输出距离在可计算的界限内。针对线性系统和卡尔曼估计器，研究提供了系统的函数构建与控制设计方法，并在无人机和六轴飞行器场景中验证了其有效性，填补了部分观测随机系统分层控制的理论空白。
papers.abstract_en: Layered control is essential for managing complexity in large-scale systems, employing progressively coarser models at higher layers. While significant advances have been made for fully observable systems, the theoretical foundations of layered control under partial observations and stochastic noise remain underexplored. To address this gap, we propose a principled layered control framework for such settings. Given a state estimator at each layer, our approach ensures that the expected output distance between systems at successive layers remains within a priori computable bounds. This is achieved by introducing a novel notion of stochastic simulation functions for partially observed systems. For the class of linear systems with Kalman estimators, we provide a systematic construction of these functions along with the corresponding control design. We demonstrate our framework on two aerial robotic scenarios: an unmanned aerial vehicle and a hexacopter with a camera payload.

papers.title_en: Complementarity by Construction: A Lie-Group Approach to Solving Quadratic Programs with Linear Complementarity Constraints
papers.authors: Arun L. Bishop, Micah I. Reich, Zachary Manchester
papers.evidence: 处理连续动力学与离散事件优化的李群方法
papers.tldr: 针对机器人领域中涉及接触力学的线性互补二次规划（LCQP）难题，本文提出了一种基于李群理论的新型求解方法。研究发现互补约束在无穷小松弛下构成李群结构，据此开发了流形优化算法。通过引入数值稳定的回拉映射，使约束在构造时即被满足，有效解决了LCQP的非凸性及收敛难点。开源求解器Marble在多项基准测试中表现优异，成功解决了现有方法难以处理的复杂机器人规划问题。
papers.abstract_en: Many problems in robotics require reasoning over a mix of continuous dynamics and discrete events, such as making and breaking contact in manipulation and locomotion. These problems are locally well modeled by linear complementarity quadratic programs (LCQPs), an extension to QPs that introduce complementarity constraints. While very expressive, LCQPs are non-convex, and few solvers exist for computing good local solutions for use in planning pipelines. In this work, we observe that complementarity constraints form a Lie group under infinitesimal relaxation, and leverage this structure to perform on-manifold optimization. We introduce a retraction map that is numerically well behaved, and use it to parameterize the constraints so that they are satisfied by construction. The resulting solver avoids many of the classical issues with complementarity constraints. We provide an open-source solver, Marble, that is implemented in C++ with Julia and Python bindings. We demonstrate that Marble is competitive on a suite of benchmark problems, and solves a number of robotics problems where existing approaches fail to converge.

papers.title_en: Precursors of extreme events and critical transitions
papers.evidence: 用于临界转换和李雅普诺夫向量的动力系统理论
papers.tldr: 本研究提出了一种基于动力系统的理论，用于解释和预测极端事件及关键转换。通过分析快慢非线性系统，识别出极端事件发生前的三个阶段级联过程：慢速状态、过渡状态和临界状态。基于此级联机制，研究者提出了两种预警指标，并在不同维度的系统中进行了数值验证。该方法实现了对极端事件100%的预测准确率和召回率，为基于理论基础的时间预测提供了新途径。
papers.abstract_en: We propose a theory based on dynamical systems to explain and predict the occurrence of extreme events, of which critical transitions form a subset. In fast-slow nonlinear systems, we identify a cascade of events preceding extreme events: (i) a slow regime, in which the fast covariant Lyapunov vectors (CLVs) are both tangent to the fast eigenvectors and remain transversal to the slow subspace; (ii) a transition regime, in which the fast eigenvalues become neutrally stable while the fast CLVs are no longer tangent to the fast eigenvectors; and (iii) a critical regime, in which a strong spectral gap in the eigenvalues causes both fast and slow CLVs to become tangent along the dominant fast direction, breaking the transversality between fast and slow subspaces. Building on this cascade, we propose two precursors to forewarn the occurrence of extreme events. We numerically test the theory and precursors on low- and higher-dimensional systems. The proposed precursors predict extreme events and critical transitions with 100% precision and recall. This work opens opportunities for time-forecasting extreme events using theoretically grounded precursors.

papers.title_en: Vectorizing Projection in Manifold-Constrained Motion Planning for Real-Time Whole-Body Control
papers.authors: Shrutheesh R Iyer, I-Chia Chang, Andrew Z. Liu, Yan Gu, Zachary Kingston
papers.evidence: 用于实时控制的流形约束运动规划
papers.tldr: 针对人形机器人等复杂系统在流形约束运动规划中计算耗时过长的问题，本文提出了一种基于CPU SIMD加速的并行化流形约束运动规划算法。该方法通过将投影约束满足过程转化为可并行化的结构，利用SIMD指令集显著提升了计算效率。实验表明，该方法比现有最先进技术快100-1000倍，首次实现了人形机器人全身准静态规划的实时生成，为动态环境下的实时约束运动规划提供了可能。
papers.abstract_en: Many robot planning tasks require satisfaction of one or more constraints throughout the entire trajectory. For geometric constraints, manifold-constrained motion planning algorithms are capable of planning collision-free path between start and goal configurations on the constraint submanifolds specified by task. Current state-of-the-art methods can take tens of seconds to solve these tasks for complex systems such as humanoid robots, making real-world use impractical, especially in dynamic settings. Inspired by recent advances in hardware accelerated motion planning, we present a CPU SIMD-accelerated manifold-constrained motion planner that revisits projection-based constraint satisfaction through the lens of parallelization. By transforming relevant components into parallelizable structures, we use SIMD parallelism to plan constraint satisfying solutions. Our approach achieves up to 100-1000x speed-ups over the state-of-the-art, making real-time constrained motion planning feasible for the first time. We demonstrate our planner on a real humanoid robot and show real-time whole-body quasi-static plan generation. Our work is available at https://commalab.org/papers/mcvamp/.
